data.content:
Here's my analysis. The project has these **legacy patterns**:

| # | Legacy Pattern | SDK-Style Replacement |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `ToolsVersion` + XML namespace | `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">` |
| 2 | `Import Microsoft.Common.props` / `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` | Implicit via SDK |
| 3 | `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment` | Not needed |
| 4 | `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` | `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` |
| 5 | Debug/Release conditional PropertyGroups | SDK defaults suffice |
| 6 | `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`, `Deterministic` | SDK defaults (both true) |
| 7 | Explicit `<Reference>` for System assemblies | Implicit for .NET Framework |
| 8 | Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items | SDK glob (`**/*.cs`) auto-includes |
| 9 | `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Project-file properties + auto-generation |

Now I'll apply the modernization:

arguments.old_str:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Product>LegacyApp</Product>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
    <AssemblyVersion>1.0.0.0</AssemblyVersion>
    <FileVersion>1.0.0.0</FileVersion>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a complete picture of this legacy .NET Framework project. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and explain how to modernize it to SDK-style format.

Legacy patterns identified:
1. **Old-style Project format** with `ToolsVersion` and XML namespace - replace with `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">`
2. **Import of Microsoft.Common.props and Microsoft.CSharp.targets** - handled automatically by SDK
3. **Explicit Configuration/Platform PropertyGroups** - defaults are handled by SDK
4. **ProjectGuid** - not needed in SDK-style
5. **FileAlignment** - not needed


6. **AutoGenerateBindingRedirects** - default in SDK-style
7. **Deterministic** - default true in SDK-style
8. **Debug/Release conditional PropertyGroups** - SDK provides sensible defaults
9. **Explicit System references** - implicit in .NET Framework SDK-style projects
10. **Explicit Compile includes** - SDK-style uses globbing by default to include all C# files automatically
11. **AssemblyInfo.cs** - attributes can move to project file properties and are auto-generated by SDK
12. **TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2** - becomes `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` in the simplified format

Now I'm converting the project to SDK-style, moving AssemblyInfo attributes into the project file as properties, removing the AssemblyInfo.cs file since the SDK generates these automatically, and cleaning up the Properties directory if it becomes empty.

Modernizing the .csproj...

data.content:
Done. The project went from **47 lines → 17 lines**. Here's what was removed/simplified:

- **Deleted**: `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` — metadata now lives in `<PropertyGroup>` and is auto-generated at build time.
- **Removed**: XML namespace, `ToolsVersion`, `Import` statements (SDK handles all of this).
- **Removed**: `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` (unnecessary or SDK defaults).
- **Removed**: Debug/Release conditional PropertyGroups (SDK provides identical defaults).
- **Removed**: All `<Reference>` items (implicit for .NET Framework SDK-style).
- **Removed**: All `<Compile>` items (SDK auto-globs `**/*.cs`).
- **Converted**: `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` → `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>`.
